outliers.relevance_reason: Pure outdoor adventure, but no explicit link to freedom, time, or money-as-a-tool.

Nothing better than catching giant tuna with the boys on some deserted islands… 

#fishing #fishing🐟 #catchoftheday #seafood
outliers.why_it_worked: It presents a pure, universally desirable fantasy of freedom, adventure, and friendship. The content is pure visual escapism that makes the viewer long for that exact feeling, with no complex message or sales pitch.

my_hooks: Nothing better than a Tuesday afternoon with no meetings on the calendar.
Nothing better than taking the family offline for a week, knowing the business is running itself.
Nothing better than packing the truck for a trip with zero cell service.
Nothing better than closing your laptop on a Monday because the systems are working.
Nothing better than building a business that buys back your time.
my_script_summary: BEAT 1: Cinematic opening shot of an ideal 'offline' activity. Fly fishing, hiking a ridge, loading up the truck.
BEAT 2: On-screen text appears with the hook, e.g., 'Nothing better than a Tuesday afternoon with no meetings on the calendar.'
BEAT 3: A montage of 2-3 more short, beautiful clips from the same activity, emphasizing freedom and presence.
BEAT 4: Final shot holds on a wide, peaceful landscape. The feeling is calm and freedom.

outliers.relevance_reason: Pure lifestyle/pet content, no connection to FieldWork's mission.

#dog #adventure #outdoors #dogsofinsta #animals #fishing #nature

outliers.hook_framework: Direct Offer for a Free Tool
outliers.why_it_worked: It directly targets a specific founder pain point with a tangible, fast, and free solution. The simple keyword CTA removes friction and drives massive engagement, signaling value to the algorithm.

I built a free tool that finds the single biggest bottleneck stealing your time. And shows you how to automate it.

If you feel chained to your business, I built a system that calculates your exact path to getting your time back.

Introduce the solution: I built a free diagnostic that shows you how.
Explain the function: It pinpoints the one system that will save you the most time.
State the speed and benefit: You can find your number in under 5 minutes.
CTA: Comment 'SYSTEM' and I'll send it to you.

my_hooks: I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in a business that lets you live offline vs one that chains you to a desk.
I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in installing one system vs learning ten new software tools.
I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in one AI employee that works 24/7 vs three VAs you have to manage.
I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in an automated business that buys your time back vs a manual business that just buys you a job.
I firmly believe there is a greater ROI in a system that works while you sleep vs grinding 15 hours a day.

If you’re the bottleneck in your business, you don't need more "hustle." You need a system that respects your brain.

Taki Moore is world-class at building lifestyle empires. He also has ADHD.

In this episode, we dig into why high-functioning entrepreneurs let themselves down on the "simple" stuff and how to stop trying to work like everyone else.

Real strategies for unconventional brains. No fluff.

DM "TAKI" for the link to the full conversation.

#takimoore #adhdbrain #adhdbusiness #adhdentrepreneur #neurodivergententrepreneur
outliers.hook_framework: The 'If You... You Don't Need X, You Need Y' Hook
outliers.why_it_worked: It names a specific founder pain point (being the bottleneck) and validates their struggle. It correctly diagnoses that the solution isn't more effort, but a smarter system, which resonates with overwhelmed entrepreneurs.
outliers.recommended_angle: Focus on the paradox of entrepreneurship: building a business for freedom, only to become its prisoner. Frame FieldWork not as a course, but as an escape plan powered by systems that buy back your time.
my_hooks: If your business owns your calendar, you don't need more hustle. You need a system that buys back your time.
If you have to show up for your business to make money, you don't have a business. You have a job with more steps.
You started a business to be free. Now you're working 60 hours a week. The problem isn't your work ethic. It's your operating model.
If you're the bottleneck in your own company, stop trying to work harder. Start building engines that run without you.
That business you built for freedom now feels like a prison. You don't need a bigger team. You need a better system.
my_script_summary: State the problem: Your business was supposed to create freedom, but now it owns you.
Introduce the false solution (more hustle, more hours) and reject it.
Present the real solution: A business built with systems that run themselves.
This is the 'Build Online. Live Offline' model.
It works by installing AI and automation to do the work while you're in the field.
DM me 'FIELDWORK' and I'll show you how we build it.

outliers.relevance_reason: Directly addresses FieldWork's core mission: freedom over money/status, living life on own terms, experiencing the world.
outliers.source_caption:
Most people chase money, status, or validation.
But the real goal has always been freedom.

Freedom to wake up excited.
Freedom to live life on your own terms.
Freedom to experience this world deeply, wildly, beautifully.

Never lose sight of that. We’re taking our lives back in the most beautiful way possible.
outliers.why_it_worked: It validates a common feeling that the typical metrics of success (money, status) are hollow. It gives the audience permission to want something deeper (freedom) and frames it as the more enlightened goal.
outliers.recommended_angle: Translate the abstract concept of 'freedom' into Christian's tangible 'Build Online. Live Offline.' framework. Position the online business not as the prize, but as the tool that buys back time for an offline life.
my_hooks: Most founders chase another million in revenue. The real goal is another thousand hours offline.
Everyone is trying to build a bigger business. We're trying to build a better life.
They tell you to build an online business. They forget it's supposed to fund your offline life.
Hustle culture sells you on the grind. The real prize was always the time back.
Stop chasing the perfect funnel. Start chasing the perfect Tuesday afternoon.
my_script_summary: Most people build businesses that trap them.
But the real goal has always been freedom.
Freedom to be unreachable.
Freedom to be fully present.
Freedom to build a life, not just a living.
That's the point of building systems.
Build online. Live offline.

outliers.relevance_reason: Emphasizes building a 'business you actually want to run,' directly aligning with designing life around what matters and money as a tool for lifestyle.

The goal isn’t to build a business that looks impressive. It’s to build one you actually want to run.

@takimoore has built one of the most respected coaching businesses in the world, having helped create more seven-figure coaches than anyone in the game. 

He breaks down why he doesn’t want to go viral, what’s actually working in the business of coaching right now and how to build a business that supports your life rather than swallowing it whole.

outliers.why_it_worked: It bypasses hustle culture trends and validates a founder's private goal: building a business that serves their life, not one that consumes it. The message resonates with a desire for sustainable, intentional entrepreneurship.
outliers.recommended_angle: Frame the business not as the end goal, but as a tool. It's an engine you build once to generate the time and money needed to live a life offline, in the field, doing what you love.
my_hooks: The goal isn't to build a business that looks impressive. It's to build one that lets you disappear.
The point of building online isn't to be online. It's to get offline.
Stop building a business that consumes your life. Start building one that funds it.
Most founders build a business that needs them. I build systems that run without me.
The goal isn't a higher monthly recurring revenue. It's more time in the mountains.
my_script_summary: The goal isn't to build a business that consumes your life. It's to build one that funds it.
I used automation to help 117 brands generate $220M. Then I used it to buy back my own time.
I'll break down how to stop trading your hours for dollars, the AI systems that can run a business for you, and how to build online so you can finally live offline.
Comment 'FIELDWORK' and I'll send you the free guide.

outliers.relevance_reason: Primarily discusses sales team strategy and sales calls, with no clear link to time freedom or lifestyle.
outliers.source_caption:
I locked my 9-person sales team in a cupboard for 90 days.⁠
⁠
Full pay. No calls. No DMs. No follow-up. No “just checking in…”⁠
⁠
It sounds unhinged.⁠
⁠
But it proved something important:⁠
⁠
The best prospects don’t want a sales call.⁠
⁠
They want clarity.⁠
⁠
They want confidence.⁠
⁠
They want an offer that makes sense and a simple way to say yes.⁠
⁠
First month: 12 clients.⁠
⁠
A few months later: 30 a month.⁠
⁠
Now we cap it at 45.⁠
⁠
No sales team heroics.⁠
⁠
No endless calls.⁠
⁠
No chasing people who were never gonna buy.⁠
⁠
If you want the deets on how this works, comment SALESLESS.
outliers.why_it_worked: It uses a shocking, paradoxical statement to create massive curiosity. The story promises a way to achieve a desirable outcome by eliminating a seemingly essential business function.
outliers.recommended_angle: Christian should frame his version around a personal experiment where he deliberately removed himself from his business to test his systems. This directly proves the 'Build Online. Live Offline' concept by showing the business runs even when he's in the field.
my_hooks: I went off-grid for 30 days to see if my business would die.
I fired myself from my own company for a month. Full stop.
I took a 4-week trip with zero cell service. My business grew.
I shut down my laptop for two weeks straight during our busiest season.
I told my team not to contact me for 30 days. No texts. No calls.
my_script_summary: The provocative action: I went completely offline for a month.
The constraints: No laptop. No email. No calls. It sounds crazy.
The insight learned: Your best clients don't want you. They want a system that works.
The surprising results: We signed 4 new clients. Revenue went up 11%.
The 'without' statements: No founder bottleneck. No 2 am emails. No deals waiting on me.
The CTA: If you want to see the systems that ran it all, comment FIELDWORK.

outliers.relevance_reason: Directly mentions wanting 'more time,' which aligns with freedom of time and buying back your time.
outliers.source_caption: Comment SHOWBIZ if you want the secret to more time on brand (which spoiler alert = more clients).
outliers.why_it_worked: It makes a direct, low-friction offer, promising a 'secret' solution to a universal pain point (lack of time) and connects it to the ultimate business goal (more clients). The keyword comment is a simple, proven mechanism for lead generation.
outliers.recommended_angle: Adopt the exact structure but pivot the ultimate benefit from 'more clients' to 'more freedom'. Frame the secret not as a way to work more, but as a system that works for you so you can live offline.
my_hooks: Comment FIELD if you want the secret to an extra 15 hours a week (which means more time away from your desk).
Comment SYSTEM if you want the secret to a business that runs itself (which spoiler alert: means you don't have to).
Comment OFFLINE if you want the secret to closing clients while you're out of office (which is the entire point of building online).
Comment ENGINE if you want the secret to making money while you're on a mountain (so your business finally serves your life).
Comment AI if you want the secret to an employee that never clocks out (which means you finally can).
my_script_summary: Hook: The secret to getting paid while you're completely offline.
Problem: It's not about working harder or finding more clients.
Reframe: It's about building an engine that works without you.
Mechanism: A system that finds leads and answers questions 24/7.
Payoff: So you can get back to the field.
CTA: Comment 'FIELD' and I'll show you how it works.

outliers.relevance_reason: Contrasts '75 Hard' content with '363 Easy' systems, promoting a less demanding approach to work, aligning with working less and escaping the grind.
outliers.source_caption:
Some coaches are trying to 75 Hard their content.⁠
⁠
I’m trying to 363 Easy.⁠
⁠
Because if it needs willpower and discipline every day… ⁠
⁠
I’m cooked.⁠
⁠
Better to build a system that gives you a little nudge:⁠
⁠
Notice something. Make something.⁠
⁠
Follow for more.
outliers.why_it_worked: It uses a familiar, high-effort concept ('75 Hard') and contrasts it with a novel, low-effort one ('363 Easy'). This creates instant relatability for anyone feeling burnt out and offers a tangible alternative.
outliers.recommended_angle: Frame the default founder path as trading time for money. Position FieldWork's system-first approach as the way to use money to buy back your time, enabling the 'live offline' promise.
my_hooks: Some founders want to build a million-dollar business. I want to build a million-dollar life.
Most founders are trying to work 80 hours a week for their business. I'm trying to work 15.
Everyone is trying to out-hustle the competition. I'm trying to out-system them.
They want to build a business they can't leave. I want to build a business I don't have to run.
Some people trade their time for more money. I use money to buy back my time.
my_script_summary: Some founders are trying to build a million-dollar business.
I'm trying to build a million-dollar life.
Because if your business needs you every day... you don't own a business. You own a job.
Better to build a system that works for you.
Build online. Live offline.
Comment 'FieldWork' and I'll show you how.

outliers.relevance_reason: Discusses building a coaching business with retention and relationships, aligning with online business building.
outliers.source_caption:
Part 3 of 5 — You do not have to force pay-in-full to build a great coaching business.⁠
⁠
A lot of people treat the sale like the finish line.⁠
⁠
It’s not.⁠
⁠
The sale is the start.⁠
⁠
If your business is built around real retention, real results, and real relationships… then the goal isn’t just to collect cash.⁠
⁠
It’s to create customers who stay.⁠
⁠
People who keep getting value.⁠
⁠
People who keep winning.⁠
⁠
People who don’t want to leave because what you do actually works.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about pricing.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about renewal.⁠
⁠
That changes how you think about the whole business model.⁠
⁠
There isn’t one right way to structure offers.⁠
⁠
There’s only the way that creates the kind of business you actually want to own.⁠
⁠
Follow for part 4 tomorrow — we’re talking about why gatekeeping your best stuff is ancient history.
outliers.why_it_worked: It validates a founder's intuition by challenging a stressful industry norm. It reframes a tactical choice (pricing) into a philosophical one about building a business you actually want, which is more empowering.
outliers.recommended_angle: Challenge the 'always on' hustle culture dogma. Frame building an online business not as a commitment to be online more, but as a system to buy back your time to live offline.
my_hooks: You do not have to be 'always on' to build a great online business.
You do not have to post 3 times a day to grow your audience.
You do not have to love marketing to build a business that works.
You do not have to chain yourself to a desk to build something successful.
You do not have to answer every DM yourself to make your first million.
my_script_summary: A lot of people treat their business like a thing they have to run.
It's not.
The business should run itself.
If it's built on real systems, real automation, and real intelligence…
Then the goal isn't just to get more clients. It's to buy back your time.
That changes how you think about marketing. That changes how you think about sales.
That changes how you think about your entire life.
There's only the way that lets you build online, so you can live offline.

outliers.relevance_reason: Strong emphasis on intentional living, nature, relationships, and slowing down, aligning with designing life around what matters and living offline.
outliers.source_caption:
What a man really needs isn’t distraction it’s reconnection.
Reconnect with nature. Train your body hard. Spend time with good friends who bring out the best in you. Eat real food, slow down, heal properly.

Then, when your spirit feels grounded again, go find a woman you can truly build with.
outliers.hook_framework: The 'It's Not X, It's Y' Framework
outliers.why_it_worked: It speaks to a deep, unspoken need for meaning beyond surface-level goals. It offers a simple, actionable prescription for a more grounded life, which feels both aspirational and achievable.
outliers.recommended_angle: Bridge the gap between the online business and the offline life it's meant to fund. Frame the business not as the end goal, but as the engine that buys back your time for the things that actually matter.
my_hooks: What a founder really needs isn't more revenue. It's more freedom.
The goal was never to work more. It was to build more.
What you really want isn't a bigger business. It's a bigger life.
The endgame isn't a perfect dashboard. It's a passport.
You don't need another tactic. You need a system that buys back your time.
my_script_summary: Hook: What a founder really needs isn't more revenue. It's more freedom.
Install the systems. Automate the workflows. Hire the AI employees.
So you can reconnect with nature. Train your body. Be fully present with your friends and family.
Then, when your business runs without you, you can go live the life you were building it for.

outliers.relevance_reason: Strong emphasis on intentional living, nature, well-being, and relationships, aligning with designing life around what matters and living offline.
outliers.source_caption:
Being “High test” to us isn’t about ego. 
It’s about how you live.

Getting sunlight. Training hard. Sauna, cold plunges, good food, good people. 
Respecting women. Calling your mum. Looking after your mates. 
Getting out in nature. Pushing yourself. Becoming harder to break and easier to love.

This isn’t about being better than anyone else. 
It’s about building a life that makes you feel alive and bringing good people along for the ride.

Thx my boy for the Inspo - @oscar14rush
outliers.why_it_worked: It reframes a term often associated with ego ('High test') into a set of wholesome, aspirational values. This allows the audience to identify with a positive, attainable vision of masculinity and life design.
outliers.recommended_angle: Redefine 'rich' away from a bank account number and toward time freedom and presence. Frame the online business as the engine that powers a deeply fulfilling offline life.
my_hooks: Being 'rich' to us isn't about the number in your bank account.
My definition of a successful business has nothing to do with revenue.
Most founders get the definition of 'wealthy' completely wrong.
This is what real freedom looks like. It's not what you think.
We're not building online businesses to escape our lives. We're building them to live.
my_script_summary: Hook: Being 'rich' isn't about your bank account. It's about how you live.
Beat 1: Show a rapid list of offline activities: Tuesday morning hikes. Fishing with your dad. Dinners with no phones.
Beat 2: Show a list of relationship-focused actions: Being present for your kids. Calling your parents. Helping a friend move.
Beat 3: Connect it to the 'how': Building a business that doesn't break you. Designing a life you don't need a vacation from.
Beat 4: State what it is NOT about: This isn't about Lambos or private jets.
Beat 5: State the core mission: It's about building a business online, so you can actually live your life offline.
